task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenCAD login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to http://localhost displaying the OpenCAD login screen.

task_completion.description: Log in using the administrator credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login (optionally showing the admin user profile or email 'admin@opencad.local').

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Data Manager or Warrant Types management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser displays the OpenCAD Data Manager, specifically the section for managing or viewing 'Warrant Types'.

task_completion.description: Enter the new warrant type name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Failure to Appear' is typed into the input field for adding a new warrant type.

task_completion.description: Successfully add and display the new warrant type
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Failure to Appear' is visible as a saved entry in the list or table of existing warrant types, or a success notification is displayed confirming its addition.

integrity.description: The agent must use the web UI, not direct database commands
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing MySQL commands like 'INSERT INTO warrant_types'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine OpenCAD application
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions take place within a web browser navigating the actual OpenCAD interface, not a text editor or mock application.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the result using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) should not be open and used to manually edit the HTML DOM to display 'Failure to Appear'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenCAD administrator credentials are Email: 'admin@opencad.local' and Password: 'Admin123!'. The exact warrant type to be added is 'Failure to Appear'. This addition must be performed through the OpenCAD web interface (typically via the Data Manager), and should appear in the system's warrant types list.
